title: OpenLore MCP server — security capability declaration
description: Machine-readable declaration of the server's security-relevant capabilities (filesystem, subprocess, network, credentials, local daemon) plus an accepted-risk register of benign source->sink patterns a generic scanner may flag. Kept in sync with the code by security-capabilities.test.ts. See openspec/specs/mcp-security/spec.md.

reads.what: source files for static analysis (tree-sitter parse, call graph, signatures)
analysis artifacts (llm-context.json, mapping.json), SQLite edge store, config.json, decisions, memory, serve.json — treated as untrusted, size-bounded, shape-validated, fail-closed
spec.md domains, change proposals, decision ADRs
read indirectly via the git subprocess (history, diffs, change coupling)

writes.what: analysis cache, edge store, decisions store, memory notes, telemetry JSONL, serve.json discovery file
synced spec decisions (append-only to pre-existing specs), change proposals (sanitized slug), decision ADRs (kebab-cased filenames)
post-commit / pre-commit hook install — opt-in via explicit --install-hook only

filesystem.confinement: Every tool path argument is confined to the validated project root via validateDirectory + safeJoin, which enforce canonical (symlink-resolved) containment. Mutators write only within .openlore/ and openspec/.

spawns.how: execFile/execFileSync/spawnSync with an argv array (no shell); refs validated against argument injection (leading-dash + shell-meta)
spawns.why: history, diffs, change coupling, drift detection

spawns.how: spawn(process.execPath | resolved bin, ['decisions','--consolidate'|'analyze']) with fixed argv
execFileSync(bin, argv) with fixed argv; bin overridable by env (CLAUDE/GEMINI_CLI/MISTRAL_VIBE_CLI)
spawns.why: optional CLI-based LLM provider transport (alternative to HTTP)

subprocess.method: argv arrays only; no shell:true and no shell-binary (-c) invocation anywhere on the core+cli server surface.
subprocess.exception: src/pi (the VS Code extension launcher) uses a Windows-only shell:true with FIXED arguments — see acceptedRisks.

egress.source: baseUrl from config / provider env vars
egress.data: prompts derived from analysis + (for LLM-backed tools) repo-derived content
text to embed for semantic search

network.prohibited: No telemetry, analytics, update-check, or any other ad-hoc network sink. Repo content is sent only to the configured provider on LLM-backed tool calls.

credentials.source: environment variables / config (provider API keys, embedding key, optional serve token)
credentials.use: authenticate the configured provider request (and gate the local daemon) only
credentials.confinement: redacted from every output channel — tool results, errors, telemetry, logs — via secret-redaction (shared with sanitizeMcpError).

localDaemon.auth: optional x-openlore-token compared in constant time; REQUIRED to bind any non-loopback host (refuses to start otherwise)
localDaemon.defense: DNS-rebinding guard (Host header must be a loopback name or the bound host) + cross-site Origin rejection, enforced before any dispatch

acceptedRisks.why: Reading the analyzed repository and invoking git over it is the tool's core purpose. git is spawned with an argv array (never a shell) and refs are validated, so no source or repo content can be reinterpreted as a command.

acceptedRisks.pattern: read config/env (baseUrl, apiKey) -> fetch(configured LLM/embedding endpoint)
acceptedRisks.why: LLM-backed tools must reach the operator's configured provider with the operator's own credentials. Egress is restricted to the configured endpoint (enumerated + asserted); no other destination receives repo-derived data.

On-disk artifacts are treated as untrusted: reads are size-bounded (ARTIFACT_MAX_BYTES), the top-level shape is validated, and parsing fails closed to a 're-run analyze' result. A poisoned artifact cannot crash the server or be emitted as authoritative.

acceptedRisks.pattern: fixed constant argv -> spawn(..., { shell: true }) in src/pi/extension.ts
acceptedRisks.why: The VS Code extension launches the openlore CLI on Windows with shell:true and a FIXED argv ('openlore', ['serve', ...]) — no untrusted value is interpolated into the command. It is the editor-integration launcher, outside the analyzed MCP server surface (src/core, src/cli) that the no-shell gate scans.

acceptedRisks.pattern: git stdout/stderr -> openSync temp file fds (was: /bin/sh -c redirect)
acceptedRisks.why: Inside the MCP server, libuv pipe() fails EBADF because FD 0/1 are the JSON-RPC sockets. git output is redirected to temp-file descriptors instead of pipes — without a shell — so the workaround introduces no command-string interpolation.
